subject: GRB 220310B: Fermi GBM Final Real-time Localization
body:
The Fermi GBM team reports the detection of a likely LONG GRB

At 02:55:07 UT on 10 Mar 2022, the Fermi Gamma-ray Burst Monitor (GBM) triggered and located GRB 220310B (trigger 668573712.866702 / 220310122).

The on-ground calculated location, using the Fermi GBM trigger data, is RA = 63.8, Dec = 69.6 (J2000 degrees, equivalent to J2000 04h 15m, 69d 35'), with a statistical uncertainty of 5.7 degrees.

The angle from the Fermi LAT boresight is 128.0 degrees.

The skymap can be found here:
https://heasarc.gsfc.nasa.gov/FTP/fermi/data/gbm/triggers/2022/bn220310122/quicklook/glg_skymap_all_bn220310122.png

The HEALPix FITS file, including the estimated localization systematic, can be found here:
https://heasarc.gsfc.nasa.gov/FTP/fermi/data/gbm/triggers/2022/bn220310122/quicklook/glg_healpix_all_bn220310122.fit

The GBM light curve can be found here:
https://heasarc.gsfc.nasa.gov/FTP/fermi/data/gbm/triggers/2022/bn220310122/quicklook/glg_lc_medres34_bn220310122.gif
